overview.heading: Topics
overview.subtitle: The same story, told ten different ways. 逆風 gathers scattered reports into one clear timeline and rates every source for credibility — so you can skip the noise and see exactly what happened.
overview.empty: No topics match the current filters

topic.searchEmpty: No events match the current search

event.researchingHint: This event is still being cross-checked across sources. Check back soon for the full background and ranked sources.

log.heading: Log
log.pageIntro: 逆風 continuously monitors a range of public news and financial-information platforms, periodically compiling their publicly published headlines and summaries through an automated process, as reference material for later cross-source comparison, fact-checking, and credibility assessment. Below is the daily activity log — click into a day to see its sources and AI research in detail.
log.summaryLine: Compiled {{articleCount}} reference items ‧ researched {{researchCount}} events in depth
log.aiEmpty: No AI research activity that day

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to 逆風 ("the Service"). These Terms of Service describe the nature of the Service, its content sources, and rules for use. By using the Service, you agree to the terms below. This page is a general statement, not individualized legal advice — please consult qualified counsel for specific legal disputes.
terms.natureBody: The Service is a news-event organization and credibility-assessment tool that automatically tracks multiple public news and financial-information platforms, arranges reports describing the same event into a timeline, and provides credibility assessments based on a public methodology. The Service is not itself a news publisher and does not publish original news content; all timeline summaries and credibility scores are generated by the system according to its stated methodology, may contain errors or delays, are provided for reference only, and constitute no warranty of any kind.
terms.copyrightBody: For each original report, the Service provides only the headline, a summary written by the Service itself, and a hyperlink to the original article — it does not reproduce or repost the full original text. This approach relies on Taiwan's Copyright Act Article 52 (quotation of already-published works, within reasonable limits, for reporting, commentary, or other legitimate purposes) and Article 61 (public transmission online of commentary on current political, economic, or social affairs, unless the copyright holder has stated that reproduction or transmission is not permitted). If a copyright holder does not wish their commentary on current affairs to be compiled and presented by the Service, they may state that reproduction is not permitted under the proviso of Article 61, or contact the Service as described in Section 9 below so the matter can be addressed. A plain hyperlink to a third-party site is not considered reproduction or distribution of that site's content.
terms.credibilityBody: Credibility ratings and tiers on the Service are assessments of the verification results for an individual report or an individual claim, based on a public methodology considering factors such as how official or named the source is and cross-source corroboration. These ratings do not represent, and do not constitute, a negative assessment of the overall character, reputation, or goodwill of any media organization, journalist, or individual. Ratings may change as verification progresses or the methodology is updated.
terms.disclaimerBody: The Service is provided "as is," with no express or implied warranty as to the accuracy, timeliness, or completeness of its content. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Service is not liable for any direct or indirect loss arising from use of, or inability to use, the Service. Event timelines and source rankings are generated automatically or semi-automatically from public information and may diverge from fact due to source errors, AI misjudgment, or delayed updates; users should independently verify content before citing or repeating it.
terms.privacyBody: The Service currently offers no account registration or login, and does not actively collect personal data such as names or contact details. Interface preferences (e.g., language, light/dark mode) and browsing history are stored only on the user's own device (localStorage), are never transmitted to a server, and are not used to identify any individual. If the Service later adds features involving the collection, processing, or use of personal data, a privacy policy will be published separately in accordance with the then-current Personal Data Protection Act (and its subsequent amendments and implementing regulations), including data-security and, where applicable, breach-notification obligations.
terms.conductBody: Users must use the Service lawfully and must not: bulk-scrape the Service's content by automated means for separate commercial use, circumvent or disrupt the Service's technical protection measures, or use the Service's content as a basis for defaming or harassing others. The Service reserves the right to restrict or terminate access where abuse is identified.
terms.changesBody: The Service may revise these terms at any time due to regulatory changes, feature updates, or other reasonable cause; revised terms will be posted on this page with an updated "last updated" date above. Continued use of the Service after a revision constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
terms.governingLawBody: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of China (Taiwan). Any dispute arising from the Service shall be subject to the Taipei District Court as the court of first instance, except where mandatory law provides otherwise.
terms.contactBody: The Service is currently operated by an individual as a utility tool and does not yet offer a formal support mailbox or online appeal form. For questions about how content sources are handled or about these terms, please watch for contact channels the Service may announce in the future.
